Land Rover Defender D250 Engine Codes

mainEntity.name: How much does a Land Rover Defender D250 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned Defender D250 (AJ20D6) engine typically costs £4,200–£5,800 supply only. Fitting adds £1,000–£1,600 depending on workshop location. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

mainEntity.name: Is the Defender D250 engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The AJ20D6 is JLR’s 3.0-litre inline-six diesel, introduced in 2021 to replace the four-cylinder Ingenium diesel. Early indications are positive — the straight-six is inherently better balanced and shares fewer of the 2.0-litre’s well-documented timing chain and oil dilution problems. A 2026 recall affecting certain AJ20-D6 engines involves the FEAD belt idler pulley — verify recall completion.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Defender D250?

acceptedAnswer.text: The primary known issue is a recall on certain AJ20-D6 engines where the front-end auxiliary drive belt idler pulley can over-rotate after high-torsion input and damage the oil pressure/temperature sensor, causing an oil leak. DPF clogging is also a concern for urban-driven examples — regular motorway running is essential.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Defender D250 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value. Against a well-kept £25,000 Defender D250, a £4,800 recon is under 20% of the car’s value — worth it. Even against a higher-mileage £18,000 example, the same recon sits well below our 60% Land Rover threshold. These vehicles hold strong value.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Defender D250 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The D250 uses the AJ20D6 (also known as DT306) — a 3.0-litre inline-six diesel with 48V mild-hybrid assistance. Production began in 2020/2021. Tell us your registration date and we’ll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the FEAD pulley/sensor issue, or does it need engine replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — this is a recall fix, not an engine replacement scenario. The front-end auxiliary drive belt idler pulley and oil pressure/temperature sensor can be replaced (£200–£400 or free under recall if your vehicle is affected). At the first sign of an oil leak or pressure warning, verify recall status immediately.

replacementCosts.h2: Land Rover Defender D250 Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the current L663-generation AJ20D6 engine. The D250 shares its engine block with D200 and D300 variants — costs are similar across the range.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a Defender D250 engine typically takes 12–16 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer).

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Defender D250 — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Catch it early — recall fix is straightforward
Regular maintenance item on urban-driven diesels
Viable if caught before debris damages engine
Little is left to repair — straight-six design is generally robust

buyingChecks.title: Buying This Defender D250? Check These First
buyIf: * Recall completed or verified not affected (FEAD pulley/sensor)
* Documented DPF maintenance history (motorway runs, regen cycles)
* Full service history with sub-15,000-mile oil intervals
* 2021-onward build — the straight-six is a meaningful upgrade over the four-cylinder diesel
avoidIf: * Unresolved oil pressure warning light or visible oil leak
* No proof of recall status on 2024–2027 models
* Poor service history — the Ingenium range is unforgiving of neglect
* Engine warning light with no diagnosis

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The Defender D250’s AJ20D6 represents a genuine step forward from the four-cylinder Ingenium diesel that preceded it. The straight-six is smoother, more refined, and carries fewer of the 2.0-litre’s timing chain and oil dilution problems. That said, it’s still a modern, emissions-controlled diesel — DPF maintenance and recall completion are non-negotiable. Buy on documented history, not just on badge appeal.

cta.label: → Compare D250 engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Land Rover Defender D250 (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for any unusual rattle or knock on startup (straight-six should be smooth)
* Service History — including which engine era (AJ20D6 — 2021-onward) and confirmed oil-interval discipline
* Smoke Check — Blue/black smoke on startup can indicate injector or DPF issues
* Turbo Boost — Test acceleration; any lag, whistling, or grinding?
* DPF Regeneration — Check history; short journeys accelerate wear on all diesel eras
* Oil Consumption — Does it use more than 1L per 1,000 miles?
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved issues? Check recall status for FEAD pulley/sensor
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures
